education.description: Individual specialization track focusing on distributed systems, operating systems, and DevOps.

education.school: Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.description: Development and maintenance of wind and solar plants data acquisition software using C, Python, and C# for backend and JS/TS for frontend, supporting devices such as turbines, inverters, and sensors, vendor-specific and industrial protocols (e.g., Siemens, Vestas, Gamesa, Modbus). Architecture and implementation of production-ready control systems such as dynamic yaw and inverter optimization. License management, high-performance decryption, and PostgreSQL/MySQL interface in C. Redis real-time data storage and fast retrieval with non-volatile persistence. CI/CD workflow, infrastructure architecture, and shell/python scripting for environment setup, edge deployment, and operations tooling.
experience.details: Owns the data-acquisition platform for utility-scale wind and solar plants — the backend that pulls telemetry off field hardware across many vendor and industrial protocols and turns it into production-ready control.
highlights: Maintains and extends the acquisition backend in C, Python and C#, with a JS/TS frontend, across turbines, inverters and meteorological sensors.
Integrates vendor-specific and industrial protocols such as Siemens, Vestas, Gamesa and Modbus.
Designed production-ready control systems such as dynamic yaw and inverter optimization running unattended at the edge.
Implemented license management, high-performance decryption and a PostgreSQL/MySQL interface directly in C, backed by Redis for real-time storage and non-volatile persistence.
Built the CI/CD workflow, edge-deployment infrastructure and shell/Python operations tooling.
experience.reviewTodo: scaffold — details/highlights AI-drafted, refine with real specifics

experience.title: Hardware & Software Engineer
experience.description: Responsible for transitioning the prototype and its firmware into a product. New features such as Over-the-Air (OTA) updates and Power/Energy Metering. Definition of technical requirements for hardware, firmware, and software. Hardware design coordination. Scalable data-collection multithreading system development using MQTT and Rust to handle high frequency real-time data from field devices. Service manager engine architecture design (AWS & GCP) and implementation using TypeScript, Bun, Docker and K8s to ensure responsiveness and availability to grid balance signals. IBM/Andel Flex Platform integration for FCR-D market regulation.
experience.details: Building on the first-generation device, this role drove the second generation of the platform — internally Distributed System Orchestration for the Flexibility Market (BEAM v2). The focus shifted from a single device to orchestrating a responsive fleet that can react to grid-balancing signals and bid into flexibility markets in real time, while taking the prototype all the way to a shippable product.
highlights: Owned the prototype-to-product transition: defined hardware, firmware and software requirements and coordinated the hardware design.
Shipped Over-the-Air (OTA) firmware updates and on-device power/energy metering.
Built a scalable, multithreaded data-collection system in Rust over MQTT for high-frequency real-time field data.
Architected the service-manager engine on AWS and GCP using TypeScript, Bun, Docker and Kubernetes, tuned for responsiveness and availability under grid-balancing signals.
Integrated the IBM/Andel Flex platform to participate in the FCR-D frequency-regulation market.
experience.team: Worked across the hardware, firmware and cloud teams, and with the market-integration side, to take the device fleet from prototype to a product participating in FCR-D. (scaffold — refine with the real team details.)

projects.title: Distributed System Orchestration for Flexibility Market — BEAM v2

experience.title: Systems Engineer
experience.description: Firmware development using C, TypeScript and Node for API and Python for the energy simulation engine. Use of ESP32 with ESP IDF for Bluetooth, WiFi, LTE/NB-IoT, and peer-to-peer communication, alongside parsers, protocols, and machine interface. Aggregator implementation for data handling and implemented a server-side state holder to validate and manage connections between the database, simulation service, and IoT devices.
experience.details: This work formed the first generation of the platform — internally the Smart Grid Interface (BEAM v1) — the bridge between the in-home energy device and the cloud. The focus was getting reliable telemetry and control off constrained ESP32 hardware and into a backend that could simulate and act on it.
highlights: Brought up the ESP32 (ESP-IDF) connectivity stack: Bluetooth provisioning, WiFi and LTE/NB-IoT fallback, plus peer-to-peer device communication.
Wrote the firmware-side parsers and machine interface, and the protocol layer framing messages between device and backend.
Built the data aggregator and a server-side state holder that keep the database, the simulation service and the field devices consistent.
Stood up the Python energy-simulation engine behind a TypeScript/Node API.
experience.team: Small cross-functional hardware/software team — collaborated closely with the firmware, backend and energy-modelling people, alongside the hardware engineers defining the device. (scaffold — refine with the real team details.)

experience.title: Backend Developer
experience.description: Development focusing on CLI, API, backend-related code, pipeline maintenance, tests, and code quality enforcement. Docker and Kubernetes for containerization and orchestration, Python, Golang, and Typescript as languages. REST, GraphQL and gRPC APIs. Cloud services from GCP and AWS. Test-driven development (TDD).
experience.details: Backend work across CLI, API and supporting services, with ongoing ownership of the pipelines, automated tests and code-quality enforcement that keep the codebase reliable and reviewable.
highlights: Wrote services in Python, Golang and TypeScript, containerised and orchestrated with Docker and Kubernetes.
Built and consumed REST, GraphQL and gRPC APIs deployed on GCP and AWS.
Maintained the CI/CD pipelines and enforced code-quality standards across the team.
Practised test-driven development (TDD) throughout.
